Q1 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which one of the following is a disadvantage of NC machines over CNC machines?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
NC machines are more prone to part programming mistakes, whereas CNC machines being
computer controlled, avoid such works.

Q2 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is not correct about jigs and fixtures?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Option (b) is incorrect because jigs are not used for turning, milling and grinding. These operations
require fixtures, as the tool is guided by the machine itself. Jigs are used in drilling-type operations
where they hold the workpiece and guide the tool using bushes.

Q3 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Match the products in Column-I with manufacturing processes in Column-II

 
 
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Sand casting : Engine blocks, Automotive cylinder blocks.
Extrusion : Seamless tubes
Powder metallurgy : Tungsten filaments
Investment casting : Gas turbine blades

Q4 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

An annealed copper strip 225 mm wide and 25 mm thick is being rolled to a thickness of 20 mm in one pass. The roller radius is 300 mm and the rolls rotate at 100 rpm. Annealed copper has a true stress of about 80 MPa in the unconstrained condition and true stress is around 275 MPa
at a true strain of 0.223. The roll separating force at average true stress in the roll gap will be _____ MN. (Correct up to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 1.55
Solution

1.55 (1.5 - 1.6)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

In a sand casting of a rectangular slab, the thickness of the solidified layer after 2 minutes is known to be 3 cm. Based on Chvorinov’s rule, the solidified thickness (in cm) after 4 minutes is _______. (Correct upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 4.24
Solution

4.24 (4.20 - 4.30)

 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Schematic diagram shows rolling of a slab P and Q are points on the surface of workpiece near entrance and exit respectively. N is the neutral point. With reference to the workpiece, which among the following statement is/are correct?

 
Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b)
Friction force favours the rolling operation at entry and opposes the rolling operation at exit.
Magnitude of friction force at point P is greater than that at Q, that’s why the rolling operation is
possible.

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

In an orthogonal cutting process, rake angle α is 6° and the width of cut, b is 10 mm. The undeformed chip thickness is 200 μm. If 13.36 gram of steel chips with a total length of 500 mm are obtained, then the slip plane angle is _____ degrees. [Take density of steel as 7.83 gm/cm3]
[Rounded off to the nearest integer]

NAT
Correct answer: 32
Solution

32 (31 - 33)

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

For a given surface, the sampling length is 0.6 mm and the graph drawn to a vertical magnification of 14000 and horizontal magnification of 120 and the areas above and below the datum line are 150, 100, 190, 60 mm2 and 90, 60, 180, 170 mm2 respectively. The value of centre line average (CLA) for the surface would be ________ μm. (Round off to nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 1
Solution

1 (1 - 1)

A metallic strip having a thickness of 12 mm is to be rolled using two steel rolls, each of 800 mm diameter. It is assumed that there is no change in width of the strip during rolling. In order to achieve 10% reduction in cross-sectional area of the strip after rolling, the angle subtended by
the deformation zone at the centre of the roll is ____ degrees. (Correct up to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 3.14
Solution

3.14 (3.0 - 3.5)

A tensile specimen with a 12 mm initial diameter and 50 mm gage length reaches maximum load at 90 kN and fractures at 70 kN. The minimum diameter at fracture is 10 mm. The true fracture stress and engineering strain at fracture are

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q13 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Amongst the following, the incorrect statements regarding an ideal cutting fluid is

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b)
An ideal cutting fluid should
(i) have a large specific heat and thermal conductivity.
(ii) have a low viscosity and small molecular size (to help rapid penetration to the chip tool
interface).
(iii) contain a suitable reactive constituent (for forming a low strength compound after reacting
with the work material).
(iv) be non poisonous and non corrosive.
(v) be inexpensive and easily available.

Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following defects are associated with welding processes?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(a, b, c)
Earring defect is associated with metal forming processes such as sheet metal forming, deep
drawing, rolling etc. Therefore, option (d) is not correct.

Q18 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Match List-I (Machining Process) with List-II (Application) and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:

 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
EDM: Shaping of hard metal or alloy steel reshaping of cemented carbide dies, used for moulding,
forging, extrusion , wire drawing.
LBM : Micro-drilling and micro welding of material.
USM : Ultrasonic machining is best process for making hole in glass. It can be used for drilling
precise hole and ductility in hand and brittle material.
ECM : Shaping of hard metal on reshaping of cemented combine tools.

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, d)

NAT
Correct answer: 29.4
Solution

29.4 (29.3 - 29.5)

height to diameter ratio for the riser to be 1, the diameter of the riser is _______ cm. (Correct upto 2 decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 8.80
Solution

8.80 (8.78 - 8.82)
